title: Dual-bin rename: add 'fabric' binary alongside permanent 'fab' alias
description: Add 'fabric' as the primary binary name in package.json bin field and update citty root command meta.name to 'fabric' so help output shows 'fabric' as the canonical name. 'fab' remains a permanent alias pointing to the same dist/index.js. Update the husky pre-commit template and any README examples that reference 'fab' to use 'fabric' primarily.

files.change: Add 'fabric': 'dist/index.js' entry alongside existing 'fab': 'dist/index.js' in the bin field so both binaries are installed.
Change defineCommand meta.name from 'fab' to 'fabric' (L14) so USAGE header shows 'fabric' in all help output.
Update FAB_BIN= idempotency check to detect both 'fabric' and 'fab' binary names so hook works after rename.
Update CLI examples to show 'fabric' as primary invocation; note 'fab' as permanent alias in a short footnote.
Mirror README.md updates — use 'fabric' in code examples, note 'fab' alias.

implementation: Open packages/cli/package.json and add 'fabric': 'dist/index.js' to the bin object (keep 'fab' entry unchanged).
Open packages/cli/src/index.ts and change meta.name from 'fab' to 'fabric' on L14.
Open templates/husky/pre-commit and update the FAB_BIN= guard to detect both 'fabric' and 'fab' (e.g. use 'which fabric || which fab' or check for either string in path).
Run 'pnpm -w build' and verify 'fabric --help' shows 'fabric' in USAGE header and 'fab --help' continues to work identically.
Search README files for 'fab ' command examples and update to 'fabric' with a note that 'fab' is a permanent alias.

reference.pattern: bin field in package.json supports multiple entries pointing to same dist file; citty meta.name controls USAGE display only
reference.examples: exploration-patterns.json (fab_to_fabric_cost): 'rename is a two-file atomic change'; bin field dual-entry: { 'fabric': 'dist/index.js', 'fab': 'dist/index.js' }

criteria: 'fab --help' exits 0 and output is functionally identical to 'fabric --help'
pnpm pack lists both 'fabric' and 'fab' in the bin section of packed package.json
No README file contains bare 'fab ' examples without a note about the alias
